title: Say The Line And We'll Guess The Movie (Kidding — YOU Guess The Movie)
dek: Eight lines that lived rent-free in pop culture for decades — no title, no character name, just the words that made you point at the screen.

items.reveal_text: The line almost sounded completely different — screenwriter Steven E. de Souza came up with it after bonding with Bruce Willis over old cowboy westerns and Roy Rogers' "yippee-ki-yay, kids." Willis has said he genuinely thought the joke wouldn't survive the final cut.

Arnold Schwarzenegger actually asked James Cameron if he could change it to "I will be back" because he struggled to pronounce the contraction — Cameron said no. The line went on to land at No. 37 on AFI's 100 greatest movie quotes list.

items.quote: Pop quiz, hotshot. There's a bomb on a bus.
items.reveal_text: Nobody actually agrees on who wrote it. Credited writer Graham Yost has long insisted it was uncredited script doctor Joss Whedon's line, but Whedon has flatly denied it — leaving one of the most quoted lines in action movies without a confirmed author.

He says it while tied up and captured by Viggo Tarasov's men — moments before proving, very violently, that the old assassin hasn't lost a step. It's become one of the most-edited, most-memed lines of the whole franchise online.

items.quote: If it bleeds, we can kill it.
items.reveal_text: The same movie also gave us "Get to the choppa!" — a line Schwarzenegger delivered completely straight, but his thick accent turned it into one of the most parodied readings in action-movie history.

items.quote: I'm too old for this s***!
items.reveal_text: Roger Murtaugh mutters some version of this catchphrase in every single Lethal Weapon movie, always right as he's about to get dragged into more chaos with his retirement still nowhere in sight.

items.quote: Your mission, should you choose to accept it...
items.reveal_text: The line is a direct callback to the original 1960s TV series, where the same "tape" briefing and self-destruct warning kicked off nearly every episode — the movies just swapped the cassette tape for whatever tech was current.

items.quote: Oh, what a day. What a lovely day!
items.reveal_text: Nux shouts it while driving straight into a massive sandstorm that's killing his fellow War Boys around him — because he's been raised to treat a glorious death as the ultimate reward. Somehow it's one of the most quoted lines from a movie that's mostly just screaming and fire.
